rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1C. Defendant Warren Pumps LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 28. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1B. Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff. Specifically, plaintiff failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that the products or materials to which it exposed plaintiff contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 1A. Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability and taken off calendar as to strict liability. Defendant did not satisfy its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. Assuming without deciding that the declarations of Mr. Singletary, Mr. Valentine and Mr. Kuhn are admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony they merely create a triable issue whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Those declarations do not sustain defendant¿s burden of demonstrating that defendant never performed work at Shell Oil that involved the disturbance of asbestos-containing materials. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. (Line 1A, Part I - Tentative ruling to be continued.)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Line 1D (Part 1). Defendant Cherne Contracting Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims. Assuming without deciding that the declarations submitted with defendant¿s moving papers of Ms. Abrahamson and Mr. Koenen are admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff¿s deposition testimony they merely create a triable issue whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Defendant¿s moving papers did not make an issue of anything other than whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame and thus that was the only issue plaintiff had to respond to. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. (Tentative ruling to be continued.)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD SANDERS et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 6, [PART ONE] Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and denied as to issue 3 (punitive damages). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiffs¿ late evidence will not be considered by the court, thus plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Pursuant to the court¿s order granting preference, plaintiffs¿ opposition was due by August 29, five court days prior to the hearing. Plaintiffs¿ opposition was not served until August 30. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) The court notes that even if it had been able to consider plaintiffs¿ evidence, plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Although the court could not consider plaintiffs¿ late evidence attached to their opposition, the court did consider the opposition brief. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am, not 9:30am. [CONTINUED ON NEXT LINE, SEE PART TWO] = (503/TLJ)
